business_ideas.idea: Lambda School model for executive assistants and office administrators, three-month bootcamp with income share agreements
Curated vendors list for copywriters, vetted and approved professionals
Credentialing agency selling IQ test certificates to top percentile individuals
Developer competency certification platform, $39 paid credential for language mastery
Athletic testing facility in multiple cities offering standardized benchmark scores for youth athletes
Chess tutoring service with annual benchmark testing and custom scores for students
Cryptographic seal certification business validating unedited media to combat deep fakes

predictions.text: Deep fakes will become a massive problem for evidence, slander, and misinformation at scale
Phone makers and camera manufacturers will need to implement cryptographic seals on all captured media

dynamics: Sam and Shaan riff together in equal partnership, bouncing ideas off each other. Sam drives some tangents (deep fakes, tech frustrations) while Shaan systematizes the business angles. High-energy banter with minimal friction; they build on each other's thoughts.
frameworks: Income share agreement model: educators profit only when graduates earn above a threshold, aligning incentives
Credentialing as a service: selling trust and social proof rather than providing tangible goods
Diversified conglomerate strategy: building portfolio over 50-100 year horizons, not quarterly cycles
learnings: Low-cost training paired with income share agreements reduces friction by eliminating upfront student costs
Credentialing businesses capture enormous value by being the sole arbiter of professional standards
Building owned platforms (email, proprietary channels) creates defensible moats vs. relying on third-party algorithms
Using persuasion and communication skills ethically depends on intent; if helping people, maximum effort is justified

banger_quotes.quote: Email list is our pirate ship and every email is a little bit of win in our sales
I want to make my own pirate ship and I want to get my pirate to be a big fucking pirate ship
If your intentions are good, then it's all fair game

numbers_dropped: 600,000 office administrators and secretaries in the US
Lambda School training cost: ~$3,000 per candidate
Chanel acquisition price: low millions in 1960s
notable: Prescient discussion of deep fakes as an existential threat to media trust, with proposed technological solution (cryptographic seals) that became relevant years later.
